the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field rarely builds (1% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below

coachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +13.9% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

metaBuilds.title: Magical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 25 — about 64 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 0% of our best core on sustained auto DPS. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 686 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +33% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Azure Core (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 23 — about 1,003 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.1× the 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Timewarp (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 31% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 35% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Mana Catalyst” ⇒ on-hit; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field midlane 52.1% over 200,539 games, magnitude not simulated.

notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants

evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
support model counts one beneficiary and active-ability heals/shields only (passive heals, CC, and damage-reduction utility are not scored)
the sim opens on Stonewall, which the field rarely builds (0% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Argus (it never builds Azure Core, Entropy) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 28 — about 64 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 26 — about 1,003 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Unbroken Will: their 3s all-in loses 22% of its bite; you give up 0% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Narbash deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
